commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to review the draft and explicitly accept the WSL2 shell boundary before landing.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff adds a security-sensitive shell wrapper, but no copied value is interpolated into the command, tokenized URLs stay on stdin, and no dependency, permission, or supply-chain surface changes are... (sha=7204c65ccb7ad3c41cb3b5e122153317b9e51911)
commands.review_summary:
The PR prepends a WSL2-only static `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` clipboard backend and adds tests for token stdin handling and non-WSL fallback order.

PR surface: Source +6, Tests +40. Total +46 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main still uses direct `clip.exe` clipboard argv, the linked issue gives WSL2 environment details, and the PR body now includes positive Windows/WSL2 readback proof for the patched bridge; I did not personally run the failing current-main flow locally.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; maintainers need to review the draft and explicitly accept the WSL2 shell boundary before landing.

target.title: fix(infra): bridge WSL clipboard through shell
target.body:
## Summary

- route WSL2 clipboard copies through a static `/bin/sh -c` bridge to `/mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe`
- keep dashboard/token URLs on stdin instead of shell argv, and avoid Bash login/profile startup
- preserve the existing macOS, Linux, native Windows, PowerShell, and non-WSL2 fallback order

Fixes #88080.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/clipboard.test.ts src/infra/wsl.test.ts src/commands/dashboard.links.test.ts` - passed, 30 focused tests
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/infra/clipboard.ts src/infra/clipboard.test.ts` - passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings, overall patch correct 0.86
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target windows --windows-mode wsl2 --no-hydrate --no-sync --label wsl-clipboard-88365-readback-raw --idle-timeout 30m --ttl 60m --timing-json --stop-after always --shell -- 'set -euo pipefail; probe="openclaw-wsl-clipboard-88365-$(date +%s)"; printf "%s" "$probe" | /bin/sh -c "cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe"; actual=$(/mnt/c/Windows/System32/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command Get-Clipboard | tr -d "\r"); printf "expected=%s\nactual=%s\n" "$probe" "$actual"; test "$actual" = "$probe"'` - passed on AWS Windows/WSL2 Crabbox `cbx_e2fe1f6f22cc`, run `run_0c617b169c74`, slug `tidal-crab`; lease stopped cleanly
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label wsl-clipboard-88365-check-changed-rebased --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --stop-after always --shell -- 'pnpm check:changed'` - passed on AWS Linux Crabbox `cbx_14030a7e2161`, run `run_d774ca368fd9`, slug `harbor-prawn`; lease stopped cleanly

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: WSL2 dashboard clipboard copies no longer rely on direct non-shell `clip.exe` argv execution or a Bash login shell. The WSL2 path uses `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and passes the copied dashboard URL through stdin.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Windows/WSL2 host for direct clipboard write/readback; AWS Crabbox Linux for `pnpm check:changed`; focused local Vitest/lint/diff-check; branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest, oxlint, diff-check, branch autoreview, AWS Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback, and AWS Linux `pnpm check:changed` commands listed above.

Evidence after fix: the Windows/WSL2 run wrote a generated probe string through `/bin/sh -c 'cat | /mnt/c/Windows/System32/clip.exe'` and read it back with Windows PowerShell `Get-Clipboard`; expected and actual values matched exactly. The WSL2 regression test asserts copied values stay on stdin rather than argv, and the non-WSL fallback-order test keeps the existing `pbcopy`, `xclip`, `wl-copy`, `clip.exe`, and PowerShell order.

Observed result after fix: Windows/WSL2 clipboard readback passed on commit `7204c65ccb7ad3c41cb3b5e122153317b9e51911`; `pnpm check:changed` passed on AWS Linux; focused tests, lint, diff-check, and autoreview passed.

What was not tested: `openclaw dashboard --no-open` end-to-end with a real token URL on a user workstation. The runner proof directly exercised the WSL2 clipboard bridge that dashboard calls, using a non-secret generated probe value.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
